evidence: Atz et al. (2021) provide the canonical molecular-sciences review of GDL, defining the term and organizing the field around symmetry priors.
The review surveys SE(3)/E(3)-equivariant message-passing GNNs (TFN, EGNN, Cormorant, NequIP, PaiNN, SE(3)-Transformer) as a central GDL family for molecular modelling.
The review covers RNN- and transformer-based chemical language models on SMILES (and SELFIES, DeepSMILES) as one of the four representation classes of molecular GDL.

Review synthesizes evidence from quantum-chemistry, drug-discovery, and protein-structure-prediction literature that geometric priors improve molecular modelling, while documenting the inconsistent gain in drug discovery.
Review explicitly documents reliable equivariant-GNN gains for quantum chemistry but inconsistent gains for drug-discovery property prediction.
